key_changes: Created new base Prisma schema file for modular schema architecture
Configured PostgreSQL datasource with environment variable
Set up Prisma Client generator with fullTextSearch and multiSchema preview features

patterns_discovered.description: Implementation uses a merged schema pattern where individual .prisma files are combined into schema.prisma via npm run schema:merge command
All enums include descriptive comments and explicit value documentation using // comments, supporting developer understanding and maintenance
Enums are grouped by business domain (Organization, Order, Billing, etc.) making the schema logically cohesive and maintainable
Status enums like OrderStatus include state flow documentation (PENDING -> CONFIRMED -> PREPARING -> READY -> DELIVERED -> COMPLETED)
Generator explicitly enables fullTextSearch and multiSchema preview features for advanced Prisma capabilities

gotchas_discovered.description: The schema uses inline comments (// comment after values) rather than doc comments (/// before). While this works, consistency with Prisma conventions (using ///) might improve IDE documentation support
OrganizationStatus.DELETED and other deletion states rely on application logic for soft delete implementation, not database constraints
fullTextSearch and multiSchema are preview features that could change in future Prisma versions, requiring schema updates
Adding new enum values requires schema migration but no deprecation pattern is established for removing unused enum values

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created a comprehensive base Prisma schema file establishing the foundation for a modular schema architecture
achievements: Defined 20 well-documented enums covering all major application domains
Established PostgreSQL datasource configuration with environment variable
Configured Prisma Client with appropriate preview features
Created clear organizational structure for maintainability
Implemented domain-driven design principles in enum organization
Provided state flow documentation for complex workflows like OrderStatus

recommendations.recommendation: Standardize enum documentation to use doc comments (///) for better IDE support and consistency with Prisma best practices
Document the application-level logic for soft deletes and establish database constraints where possible (e.g., check constraints on status fields)
Monitor Prisma preview feature releases and plan migration strategy for fullTextSearch and multiSchema when they stabilize
Establish versioning/changelog for enum values to track deprecations and additions, especially important for breaking API changes
Link this base.prisma file in DATABASE_SCHEMA.md with examples of enum usage patterns and state machine workflows
Verify all referenced enums in subsequent schema files exist in base.prisma to prevent schema merge conflicts
